title: Warn on scheduling of non-inlined tasks/functions
body:
Verilator allows recursive functions since commit aa60847.

However, the scheduler is still unable to peek into function calls. This means impure recursive functions are not being scheduled properly.

Consider this example:

```systemverilog
module t;
    logic bar, qux;

    task foo(logic recurse);
        if (recurse) foo(0);
        else bar = ~bar;
    endtask

    assign qux = $c("1") & bar;

    initial begin
        for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++) #1 foo(1);
        #1 $finish;
    end

    always @(edge bar) $display("bar=%b", bar);
    always @(edge qux) $display("qux=%b", qux);
endmodule
```

The output is:

```
bar=1
bar=0
bar=1
bar=0
bar=1
bar=0
bar=1
bar=0
bar=1
bar=0
- example.sv:13: Verilog $finish
```

So `qux` is never written to. If you remove this part:

```systemverilog
        if (recurse) foo(0);
        else /* keep the assignment */
```

Then the result is correct.

I see two options:

* Make the scheduler look into function calls to account for side effects. Doesn't seem too hard, but non-obvious issues may crop up.
* Disallow recursive functions in synthesizable code, but keep them in verification/class code, as static scheduling doesn't affect it anyway. That way we can still have the recursion in UVM.
